title: Support On-Call Design Playbook
description: Design support on-call around three levers: compensation that feels distinct, tiering so specialized staff are not wasted on basics, and a locked escalation path from after-hours contact to the correct tier.

mindset.principle: On-call war stories are endless and mostly not the product. The product is pay, tiers, and path. If those are wrong, the nights will always be stupid.

mistakes.label: On-call with no pay or recognition difference
mistakes.cost: People feel used; quality and coverage rot

mistakes.label: Highly specialized tier answers basic how-tos after hours
mistakes.cost: Burnout and rage; real incidents queue behind trivia

mistakes.label: Anyone can page anyone with no filter
mistakes.cost: Chaos; sales and customers train on the wrong number

mistakes.label: After-hours rules live in one person's head
mistakes.cost: Breaks when that person is the one down

design_levers.label: Pay or equivalent differential so on-call feels treated differently
Clear L1/L2/L3 (or equivalent) skills and what each may take
Written after-hours path: intake filter before specialist page

decision_branches.situation: Specialists buried in basics after hours
Unclear who to call and when
Sales or VIPs skip the path and page random experts

steps.name: Publish what on-call pays or grants

agent_signal.use_when: Support on-call is broken or bitter. Need pay differential, tier protection for specialists, and locked after-hours escalation path. Not a collection of night war stories.
decision_criteria: On-call compensation or recognition must feel different.
Specialist tiers are not the how-to desk after hours.
Written path from after-hours contact to correct tier.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: validity is structure (comp, tiers, path), not endless on-call anecdotes.
agent_signal.not_for: War-story anthology. Not legal wage advice; set pay with your counsel/HR.
trigger_phrases: Support on-call is broken or bitter. Need pay differential, tier protection for
Need playbook: Support On-Call Design Playbook
